description: Machine-readable index of the Claude Code Workflow Kit's skills, permission categories, inputs, outputs, handoffs, and programmatic surfaces. Hand-maintained; validated by bin/validate-kit-json. See ADR-047 and docs/agent-contract.md.

permissionCategories.1: substitutable — local and reversible; auto-mode runs without asking
permissionCategories.2: operator-acknowledged-bypass — auto-mode proceeds but pauses to acknowledge a bypass in chat
permissionCategories.3: non-substitutable — public or hard-to-reverse; always requires explicit approval

skills.name: adr-writer
skills.description: Draft ADRs from settled architectural decisions.

inputs.name: decision-topics
inputs.description: One or more architectural decision topics (often the 'Decisions needing ADRs' list from prd-to-mvp)

inputs.name: --skip-check
inputs.description: Opt out of the /check-plan pre-write gate

outputs.description: One ADR per decision, status: proposed

skills.name: audit-milestone
skills.description: Verify a GitHub milestone is complete; advisory pass/fail.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: Milestone number or title to audit

outputs.description: Pass/fail audit with a gap list; advisory, no files written

next.when: audit passes and summary is reviewed

skills.name: changelog
skills.description: Render grouped release notes from a git commit range.

inputs.name: --from / --to
inputs.description: Commit range to render (or --since-last-release)

inputs.name: --output
inputs.description: Write to FILE instead of stdout

inputs.name: --github-release
inputs.description: Use the output as the body for GitHub Release TAG

outputs.description: Grouped release notes (or FILE / GitHub Release body)

skills.name: check-plan
skills.description: Validate an ADR or issue prompt against version-locked criteria.

inputs.name: artefact
inputs.description: Path to the ADR or prompt to validate, or - for stdin

inputs.name: --criteria-set
inputs.description: Which checklist to apply: adr or prompt

inputs.name: --format
inputs.description: Output format: text (default) or json

outputs.description: Pass/fail/warn report; advisory only, no files written

skills.name: clarify
skills.description: Resolve below-ADR gray areas into design/decisions.md (opt-in).

next.when: a decision graduates to an ADR

skills.name: claude-issue-executor
skills.description: Plan-first, test-alongside implementation for one issue.

inputs.name: prompt-or-issue
inputs.description: Path to prompts/issue-NNN-*.md, or the issue number

inputs.name: --no-prompt
inputs.description: Run without a prepared prompt file

outputs.description: Feature branch, incremental commits, tests alongside

skills.name: complete-milestone
skills.description: Close a GitHub milestone and optionally chain /release.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: Milestone number or title

inputs.name: --release / --no-release
inputs.description: Whether to chain /release on close

inputs.name: --dry-run
inputs.description: Preview without closing the milestone

skills.name: feature-prd
skills.description: Capture a major feature update as an additive PRD addendum extending design/prd.md.

inputs.name: feature
inputs.description: The major feature or capability area to plan (description, notes, or roadmap reference)

outputs.description: Numbered PRD addendum extending design/prd.md; original PRD never overwritten

next.when: the addendum surfaces decisions needing ADRs
the decomposition feeds the GitHub backlog

skills.name: idea-to-prd
skills.description: Turn a rough idea into a lightweight PRD draft.

inputs.name: idea
inputs.description: A rough idea in any form (paragraph, notes, conversation)

skills.name: issue-planner
skills.description: Turn MVP + build-out plan into a GitHub issue backlog and board.

inputs.name: --dry-run
inputs.description: Draft the issue batch without creating it

inputs.name: --no-project
inputs.description: Skip GitHub Project board creation

inputs.name: --force
inputs.description: Recreate issues even if a backlog exists

outputs.description: Batch of issues created via gh issue create

skills.name: milestone-summary
skills.description: Write a milestone retrospective summary.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: Milestone number or title

inputs.name: --dry-run
inputs.description: Preview without writing the file

inputs.name: --overwrite
inputs.description: Replace an existing summary (lessons zone preserved)

outputs.description: Milestone summary; lessons zone preserved on re-run

skills.name: pause
skills.description: Refresh design/state.md (and an optional handoff note).

inputs.name: --handoff
inputs.description: Also write notes/handoff-YYYY-MM-DD.md

skills.name: planning
skills.description: Capture deeper decomposition, risks, and sequencing (opt-in).

inputs.name: --granularity
inputs.description: Decomposition depth: coarse | standard | fine

skills.name: pr-review-packager
skills.description: Package a branch into a GitHub PR with a filled template.

inputs.name: --label / --milestone / --reviewer
inputs.description: Pass-through flags to gh pr create

inputs.name: --draft
inputs.description: Open the PR as a draft

inputs.name: --base
inputs.description: Override the base branch (default main)

outputs.description: PR opened via gh pr create

skills.name: prd-normalizer
skills.description: Rewrite any PRD or notes into the canonical normalized PRD.

inputs.name: prd
inputs.description: A PRD-like document (standard or custom shape)

skills.name: prd-to-mvp
skills.description: Scope a normalized PRD into an MVP and a phased build-out plan.

inputs.name: --granularity
inputs.description: Decomposition depth: coarse | standard | fine

outputs.description: Phased plan plus the 'Decisions needing ADRs' list

skills.name: prepare-issue
skills.description: Auto-fill an implementation prompt from a GitHub issue and ADRs.

inputs.name: issue-number
inputs.description: GitHub issue number (no #)

inputs.name: --skip-check
inputs.description: Opt out of the /check-plan pre-write gate

inputs.name: --pr-scan-limit
inputs.description: Merged-PR scan window for carry-forward notes (default 30)

skills.name: release
skills.description: Tag a semver release and publish a GitHub Release.

inputs.name: --version / --bump
inputs.description: Explicit X.Y.Z, or major | minor | patch

inputs.name: --draft / --prerelease
inputs.description: Release visibility flags

inputs.name: --dry-run
inputs.description: Preview without tagging or publishing

outputs.description: Annotated tag pushed; Release published via gh

skills.name: resume
skills.description: Brief a fresh session from design/state.md (gh fallback).

outputs.description: Phase, in-flight issue, recent PRs, blockers, next action

skills.name: review-pr
skills.description: Generate a dry-run AI review of a GitHub PR; publish comments only after explicit approval.

inputs.name: pr
inputs.description: The PR number to review

inputs.name: --profile
inputs.description: Review depth: strict | balanced (default) | lightweight

inputs.name: --publish
inputs.description: After the dry-run, offer to publish (still requires explicit approval before any GitHub write)

outputs.description: Structured dry-run review artifact in target installs (also rendered .md); local, posts nothing
Posted only after explicit approval; records a receipt under .claude/receipts/

skills.name: start
skills.description: Inspect project state and recommend (or invoke, for cat-1) the next skill; the kit's 'what now?' router. Also answers to /next.

inputs.name: goal
inputs.description: Optional one-line hint about what to do next (e.g. 'plan a feature', 'ship')

outputs.description: The next skill to run, why, and the active operating mode; may directly invoke a cat-1 target

next.when: the project has only an idea
a normalized PRD exists but no MVP
an issue is selected but has no prepared prompt
a prepared prompt exists on disk
a branch is ahead of main and ready to ship
the user wants a major feature update on a mature project

skills.name: workflow-docs
skills.description: Generate README.md, design/architecture.md, and design/ai-summary.md for the project.

bin.name: check-plan
bin.description: Validate an ADR or prompt against version-locked criteria (predates the standard envelope; see ADR-043).

bin.name: prepare-issue
bin.description: Read-only analysis of an issue's prepared prompt: path, staleness, ADR references, gaps, and next action.

bin.name: validate-kit-json
bin.description: Check kit.json agrees with skills/ frontmatter (names, permission categories, next targets).

bin.name: sync-adr-index
bin.description: Regenerate the ADR index table in design/adr/README.md (see ADR-023).

bin.name: validate-carry-forward
bin.description: Validate cross-skill carry-forward design-questions blocks against schemas/design-questions.v1.yaml; --against traces a producer file into a PR body or prompt (ADR-040, ADR-050).

bin.name: write-receipt
bin.description: Write or look up an idempotency receipt for a mutating skill under .claude/receipts/ (schemas/receipt.v1.yaml, docs/receipts.md, ADR-050).

bin.name: check-consistency
bin.description: Verify docs and metadata agree: skills.md vs kit.json, the verb layer, the bin registry, schema references, and category legend (ADR-050).

bin.name: self-test
bin.description: Run the kit's non-mutating validation surface against itself and a stub, timing it and listing friction (workflow self-test, ADR-050).

bin.name: review-pr
bin.description: Generate a DRY-RUN AI review artifact from a PR diff (gh or --diff) via an OpenAI-compatible provider (or --mock); posts nothing (ADR-051).

bin.name: publish-review
bin.description: Preview an AI review and post it to a GitHub PR only on the deterministic --confirm token; idempotent via receipts (ADR-051, ADR-050).

bin.name: review-eval
bin.description: Offline fixture harness for AI PR review: runs review-pr --mock over ai-review/eval/fixtures and checks expectations + duplicate prevention (ADR-051).

bin.name: fence
bin.description: Read and splice marker-fenced zones (state/summary/workflow-docs/adr-index) without clobbering surrounding prose; skills shell out to it (ADR-054).

bin.name: fences-eval
bin.description: Offline golden-fixture harness proving byte-stable marker-fence round-trip and splice behavior (ADR-054).

bin.name: validate-schema
bin.description: Validate a JSON data file against a kit schema (unit/required/type/const/enum/pattern) so schemas/*.yaml are executable contracts (ADR-054).
